sources.source_title: Chapter 3: Voting Rights in Mississippi Delta
sources.notes: Government civil rights history summarizing Black voter registration in Mississippi before and after the Voting Rights Act.

sources.source_title: Section 4 of the Voting Rights Act
sources.notes: DOJ overview of the coverage formula and targeted remedies used to address racial discrimination in voting.
Research explainer on the structure and significance of the Voting Rights Act and subsequent weakening by the courts.

metrics.metric_name: Black voter registration in Mississippi under the Voting Rights Act era
metrics.methodology_note: Uses Mississippi Black voter registration before the Voting Rights Act and a later preclearance-era benchmark cited in government and court materials to illustrate the scale of long-run change.

sources.source_title: Effects of Shelby County v. Holder on the Voting Rights Act
sources.notes: Research summary explaining how the ruling weakened preclearance and opened the door to restrictive voting laws.
Case overview summarizing the decision and the role of the Voting Rights Act provision that was struck down.

sources.source_title: Shelby County v. Holder Supreme Court Opinion
sources.notes: Opinion text for the 2013 decision invalidating the Section 4 coverage formula used for preclearance.
DOJ overview of the Fair Housing Act, including race discrimination in sales, rentals, lending, and enforcement.

sources.source_title: 1968 and the Beginnings of Federal Enforcement of Fair Housing
sources.notes: Historical DOJ account of early federal enforcement activity after enactment of the Fair Housing Act.
Current DOJ enforcement page covering the Fair Housing Act and related anti-discrimination authority.
National Archives milestone document page for the Brown decision and its constitutional significance.

sources.source_title: Brown v. Board of Education Lesson Page
sources.notes: Educational context page summarizing the ruling and its legal effect on school segregation.

sources.source_title: Timeline of Events Leading to Brown v. Board of Education
sources.notes: Archival teaching resource placing Brown within the longer sequence of segregation and legal challenge.
National Archives milestone document page describing the law's major anti-discrimination provisions.

sources.source_title: Title VI of the Civil Rights Act of 1964
sources.notes: DOJ overview of Title VI and the prohibition on race discrimination in federally funded programs.

sources.source_title: Civil Rights Act of 1964 Document Highlights
sources.notes: National Archives highlights page summarizing the law's scope across public accommodations, employment, and federally funded programs.
